summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard transfer of the mysterious 11/19/66 show, bursting with early‑Dead energy – Cream Puff, Hi‑Heel Sneakers and a pig‑filled set steal the spotlight.
review: The Miller‑sourced stereo soundboard tape delivers surprisingly vivid clarity for a 1966 recording, though a few listeners note channel‑swap/pitch quirks and a mid‑song cut on “Dancing in the Street.” Musically the performance is electrifying – Pigpen dominates with rare Otis Redding cover, “Cream Puff,” and an all‑Pigpen second set, while “Hi‑Heel Sneakers,” “Smokestack Lightning” and “Just a Hand to Hold” are highlighted as standout moments.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 15 reviews overwhelmingly praise both the show and Miller's transfer; only minor technical complaints lower the rating slightly.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Stereo hi‑fi mix praised as crisp and clear; occasional channel‑swap and +2 % pitch correction needed, plus a noticeable drop‑out during “Dancing in the Street.” Overall listenable and superior to most early‑Dead tapes.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Two‑set early‑Dead set, heavy on blues and Pigpen’s organ/vocals. The first set builds with “Cold Rain & Snow” and “Hi‑Heel Sneakers,” while the second set showcases an all‑Pigpen vibe culminating in a powerful “Smokestack Lightning.”

band_member_comments.Bob: A brief misstep around 3:35 noted by one reviewer.
